definition: A strong feeling of suitability for a particular career; a calling
example: Teaching was more than a job for her—it was a vocation.

definition: Speaking or spoken incessantly and fluently
example: The voluble tour guide shared fascinating stories at every stop.

definition: Playfully quaint or fanciful; acting in a capricious manner
example: The whimsical garden featured sculptures of fairy-tale characters.

definition: To hold and use a weapon or tool; to have and use power
example: The CEO wielded considerable influence in the industry.

definition: Having or showing a feeling of vague or regretful longing
example: She gave a wistful smile as she looked at old photographs.

definition: To remain undamaged or unaffected by; resist
example: The building was designed to withstand earthquakes.

definition: To have a long, complicated dispute or argument
example: The lawmakers wrangled over the details of the budget bill.

definition: Dislike of or prejudice against people from other countries
example: Xenophobia can lead to discrimination against immigrant communities.

definition: To produce or provide; to give way under pressure
example: The fertile soil yielded an abundant harvest.

definition: The time at which something is most powerful or successful
example: The empire reached its zenith during the 18th century.

definition: A soft gentle breeze
example: A warm zephyr rustled the curtains on the summer afternoon.
